sense_check_acknowledgments.question: Does send_social_picks.py:116 still compile and call correctly after the environment kwarg append? The call uses keyword args (guild_id=, live_channel_id=, limit=) so trailing environment should be fine — but did you verify?
sense_check_acknowledgments.verdict: VERIFIED. send_social_picks.py:116 calls db_handler.get_recent_live_update_feed_items(guild_id=..., live_channel_id=..., limit=...) using keyword args. The new signature has environment: str = 'prod' appended as the LAST parameter. Since all existing call args are keyword-named, the trailing defaulted kwarg is backward-compatible. File compiles cleanly (python3 ast.parse check passes).
